title: Transformer-Based Image Captioning
concept: Modern image captioning architectures use Vision Transformers (ViT) as encoders and text Transformers as decoders, replacing LSTMs entirely.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main benefit of visual attention in captioning?

options.text: It focuses the decoder on specific image regions per word, improving detail.
It makes the model run on CPUs without a GPU.
It removes the need for a text decoder.

exercises.explain: Correct! Dynamic attention focuses the decoder on relevant regions, enhancing accuracy on detailed objects.

cards.title: The Transformer revolution
cards.body: LSTMs process words sequentially, which is slow and makes it hard to remember details over long sentences. Transformers changed everything. By treating both the image patches and text words as tokens in a shared network, we can capture deep context and generate captions much faster.

cards.title: ViT-Transformer Architectures
cards.body: Modern captioning models use a Vision Transformer (ViT) encoder to split the image into patches and encode them as visual tokens. A Text Transformer Decoder processes these visual tokens alongside the generated text tokens, using cross-attention to model deep multi-modal connections.

cards.title: A round-table discussion
cards.body: Sequential models (CNN-RNN) are like a game of telephone: information is passed down a line. Transformer captioning is like a round-table meeting: the visual tokens and the text tokens are all present on the table, and everyone can look at and compare all inputs simultaneously to build the best sentence.

cards.title: Donut and BLIP models
cards.body: State-of-the-art architectures like BLIP or GIT leverage massive pre-trained vision-language models. They align image patches with text tokens, generating highly detailed captions and answering questions about the image (Visual Question Answering).

cards.title: RNN loops are not mandatory
cards.body: You might think generating text requires a recurrent loop (like LSTM). LSTMs were standard for years, but Transformers handle sequence dependencies in parallel using attention weights, eliminating recurrence bottlenecks and speeding up training dramatically.

left.text:
Sequential loop execution
Prone to forgetting early words
Slow training (non-parallel)

right.text:
Parallel attention computation
Perfect long-range context memory
Fast, scalable training

exercises.prompt: How does a Vision Transformer (ViT) encoder process an input image?

options.text: It splits the image into flat patches and processes them as sequence tokens
It converts the image into a 1D vector of coordinates
It runs NMS to crop individual faces
It applies a blur filter using classical kernels

exercises.explain: Vision Transformers divide the image into spatial patches (e.g. 16x16 pixels) and embed them as sequence tokens.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Modern Transformer captioning models can process and compare all word tokens and image patches in parallel during training.
exercises.explain: True. Transformers compute self-attention across all tokens simultaneously, enabling parallel training unlike sequential RNNs.

exercises.sentence: In modern models, the LSTM is replaced by a text Transformer ___.
exercises.explain: A Transformer decoder maps the visual tokens to output text strings.

exercises.prompt: Which of these describes a cross-attention step in Transformer-based captioning?

options.text: The text decoder queries the visual tokens from the ViT encoder to align word generation with image regions
The model runs face verification on database vectors
The CNN extracts color histograms from pixels

exercises.explain: Cross-attention allows the text decoder to query visual representations to generate accurate captions.

exercises.prompt: You are building a high-speed search engine describer that must generate detailed text captions for millions of web images daily. Which architecture should you deploy to maximize training scalability and speed?

options.text: A Transformer-based model (ViT encoder + Transformer decoder), to take advantage of parallel training and high-speed attention mechanisms.
A classical CNN-LSTM model, because LSTMs train faster on huge parallel GPU clusters.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Transformer-based models scale efficiently and train much faster than recurrent networks due to parallel attention math, making them the industry standard for large datasets. Outstanding! Next: how we evaluate generated captions.
